title: InverseMetric
summary: Specifies inverse ambient metric components and verifies the formal block inverse identities in normal coordinates.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Specifies inverse ambient metric components and verifies the formal block inverse identities in normal coordinates.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal boundary
mathematicalContent.body: This page describes exactly the declarations present in Ambient/FG/InverseMetric.lean; claims not represented by a declaration are not counted as verified.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Data/interface: Normal Inverse Metric Components. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: structure NormalInverseMetricComponents (ι : Type w) extends NormalMetricComponents ι

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: inverse Metric Comp. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def inverseMetricComp (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : AmbientIndex ι → AmbientIndex ι → ℝ | AmbientIndex.zero, AmbientIndex.zero => 0 | AmbientIndex.zero, AmbientIndex.base _ => 0 | AmbientIndex.zero, AmbientIndex.inf => C.t⁻¹ | AmbientIndex.base _, AmbientIndex.zero => 0 | AmbientIndex.base i, AmbientIndex.base j => (C.t ^ 2)⁻¹ * C.gInv i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: inverse Metric Comp zero zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_zero_zero (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.zero = 0

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: inverse Metric Comp zero inf. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_zero_inf (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.zero AmbientIndex.inf = C.t⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: inverse Metric Comp inf zero. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_inf_zero (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.zero = C.t⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: inverse Metric Comp base base. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_base_base (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) (i j : ι) : inverseMetricComp C (AmbientIndex.base i) (AmbientIndex.base j) = (C.t ^ 2)⁻¹ * C.gInv i j

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: inverse Metric Comp inf inf. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverseMetricComp_inf_inf (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : inverseMetricComp C AmbientIndex.inf AmbientIndex.inf = -2 * C.rho * (C.t ^ 2)⁻¹

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Base Left Inverse. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def BaseLeftInverse [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Definition: Base Right Inverse. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: def BaseRightInverse [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Formal proposition: inverse metric base base left. The exact mathematical type is preserved from the Lean signature shown alongside it.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inverse_metric_base_base_left [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (C : NormalInverseMetricComponents ι) (ht : C.t ≠ 0) (hbase : BaseLeftInverse C) (i j : ι) : (∑ k : ι,

references.locator: Chapters 2–3, ambient space and normal form
references.note: Geometric source for the ambient bundle, homogeneity, normal form, and commutator calculations.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
